title: I built a trading journal after my 3rd blown account. 28 traders are now using it daily  -  here's what the data is showing us
author: TradeLeap
selftext:
Third blown account in 18 months. Every time I reviewed my trades, I was doing it in a spreadsheet I half-maintained and half-abandoned.

I couldn't see patterns. I couldn't tell if my losses were coming from bad setups or bad emotions. I just had a column of red numbers.

So I built TradeLeap  -  a trading journal specifically for retail forex traders.

Not a generic note-taking app. Not another spreadsheet template. A tool where you log the asset, the result (TP/SL/BE), your PnL in euros, your emotional state at entry, and whether you actually followed your plan.

The part that surprised me: the emotion field is the one people use most.

We have 28 traders using it now. The pattern showing up across accounts  -  traders who log their emotion as "frustrated" or "euphorique" before entry have significantly worse plan adherence than when they enter as "neutre" or "confiant."

Nobody needed an AI to tell them emotions affect trading. But seeing your own data confirm it, trade by trade, is different.

The app also has an AI analysis layer that generates insights based on your logged trades  -  the more you log, the more specific the feedback gets.

Still early. 28 users. But the feedback is pushing me to keep building.

Happy to answer questions about what I built or how the analysis works.
